text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
18-MONTH ONCOLOGY SURVEILLANCE EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION:
49-year-old woman (now age 50) with history of left breast triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma, Stage IIB (T2N1M0), grade 3, treated with neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus dose-dense doxorubicin/cyclophosphamide followed by weekly paclitaxel, then left modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection and right prophylactic mastectomy with bilateral tissue expander placement. Pathology demonstrated non-pCR with residual disease (ypT1cN1a, RCB Class III). Received post-mastectomy radiation therapy, adjuvant capecitabine x 8 cycles, and 14 cycles of pembrolizumab (discontinued for grade 3 immune hepatitis). Subsequently completed 1 year of adjuvant olaparib given germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant. Tissue expanders exchanged for permanent bilateral silicone implants. Now presenting for routine 18-month post-surgical surveillance. Prior scans at 6 and 12 months post-surgery were unremarkable.

COMPARISON:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast, 12-month post-surgical surveillance (prior study)

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed in the portal venous phase following administration of 100 mL of iohexol IV contrast without adverse reaction. 5 mm axial slices with multiplanar reformats in coronal and sagittal planes. Lung windows reviewed separately.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The lungs are clear bilaterally without consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pleural effusion. The central airways are patent. A single, well-circumscribed, solid pulmonary nodule is identified in the right lower lobe at the level of the right inferior pulmonary vein, measuring approximately 4 mm in greatest dimension (image 38, series 3). The nodule is smoothly marginated with no internal cavitation, ground-glass halo, or spiculation. No satellite lesions. This nodule was NOT identified on the prior CT from approximately 6 months ago; it cannot be determined with certainty whether this represents a new lesion or one below the threshold of detection on prior imaging. No additional pulmonary nodules are identified in either lung. No endobronchial lesions.

Pleural Spaces:
No pleural effusions bilaterally. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. No anterior mediastinal mass. Thyroid gland appears grossly unremarkable on limited views.

Chest Wall and Breasts:
Bilateral silicone breast implants in place following exchange from tissue expanders, consistent with the reported surgical history. No implant rupture or periprosthetic fluid collection identified. Postoperative soft tissue changes in the left anterior chest wall and axilla are stable compared with prior, consistent with history of modified radical mastectomy and post-mastectomy radiotherapy. Mild subcutaneous fibrosis in the left chest wall soft tissues is stable and consistent with prior radiation effects. No new chest wall mass or soft tissue thickening.

Left axilla demonstrates expected post-surgical changes; no discrete lymph node mass or pathologically enlarged node identified. Right axilla unremarkable.

Bones (Chest):
No lytic or blastic osseous lesions in the imaged ribs, sternum, clavicles, or thoracic spine. No compression deformities.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal lesion. No hypodense or hypervascular masses. No biliary ductal dilatation. The hepatic parenchyma appears smooth and without nodularity. Portal vein patent. This appearance is reassuring given the history of prior immune-mediated hepatitis managed with corticosteroids; hepatic parenchyma appears entirely normal.

Gallbladder and Biliary Tree:
Gallbladder is present without wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, or gallstones. Common bile duct is not dilated.

Pancreas:
Pancreas is normal in size, morphology, and enhancement without ductal dilatation, focal lesion, or peripancreatic stranding.

Spleen:
The spleen is normal in size and homogeneous in enhancement. No focal splenic lesion.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in morphology without nodule or mass.

Kidneys and Ureters:
Both kidneys enhance symmetrically without focal mass, hydronephrosis, or perinephric abnormality. No renal or ureteral calculi identified.

Bowel:
No bowel obstruction, wall thickening, or mesenteric stranding. Appendix is visualized and appears normal.

Retroperitoneum and Lymph Nodes:
No retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or para-aortic lymphadenopathy. The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber.

Peritoneum and Ascites:
No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity.

Bones (Abdomen and Pelvis):
No suspicious lytic or blastic osseous lesions in the lumbar spine, sacrum, iliac wings, or proximal femora. Vertebral body heights maintained.

---

PELVIS:

Uterus and Adnexa:
Uterus is anteverted and appears unremarkable. No adnexal mass identified. Note is made of the patient's known BRCA2 germline mutation and ongoing discussion of risk-reducing salpingo-oophorectomy; adnexal structures appear normal on this examination.

Urinary Bladder:
Urinary bladder is partially distended and unremarkable.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. NEW 4 mm smooth, solid right lower lobe pulmonary nodule, not identified on prior CT 6 months ago. Given the nodule's small size, smooth margins, and absence of worrisome morphologic features (no spiculation, no ground-glass component), this is most likely benign in etiology -- differential includes a small granuloma, intrapulmonary lymph node, or other benign etiology. However, in the setting of a patient with a history of high-risk breast malignancy, short-interval dedicated CT chest follow-up is warranted to establish stability. Per Fleischner Society guidelines for a solid nodule of this size in a patient at elevated clinical risk, follow-up CT in approximately 3 months is recommended.

2. No evidence of locoregional breast cancer recurrence. Stable post-mastectomy and post-radiation changes in the left chest wall. Bilateral silicone implants intact without complication.

3. No hepatic lesions, no pulmonary consolidation, no mediastinal or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and no suspicious osseous lesions to suggest distant metastatic disease.

4. Liver parenchyma appears normal without residual changes of prior hepatitis.

Radiologist: [Electronic signature on file]
Attending Radiologist, Diagnostic Imaging — Body CT
Report verified and finalized.
